narrative.context: You shared an idea in your friend group chat. No one reacts for hours. You feel uneasy and start checking the chat repeatedly.
narrative.prompt: What helps you perceive what’s happening more accurately?

options.label: Name your feeling and look for evidence
options.description: Notice anxiety + uncertainty, then check contextual cues (time of day, past patterns, whether others are also quiet).

ei.rationale: Separates internal emotion signals from external evidence, improving accuracy in perceiving emotions under ambiguity.

options.label: Ask one clarifying question
options.description: Send a neutral follow-up: 'Just checking—does this idea work for everyone?'

ei.rationale: Seeks more information, but may still be driven by discomfort with uncertainty rather than careful cue-reading.

options.label: Assume they’re annoyed
options.description: Conclude you said something wrong and withdraw without checking.

ei.rationale: Misreads absence of response as negative emotion without evidence; perception becomes biased by anxiety.

options.label: Send multiple messages to force a response

ei.rationale: Escalates without understanding cues; reduces accuracy and increases social pressure.
